query: CIA Director John Ratcliffe visit to Cuba May 2026 details and any follow-up visits by US cabinet officials planned
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Ratcliffe's Cuba Visit (May 14, 2026):** CIA Director John Ratcliffe made a rare, publicly disclosed trip to Havana, meeting with Raúl Castro's grandson "Raulito" Rodríguez Castro, Interior Minister Lázaro Álvarez Casas, and Cuba's intelligence chief. He delivered Trump's message that the US would engage economically/security-wise only if Cuba makes "fundamental changes," alongside a renewed $100M humanitarian aid offer plus Starlink internet — all contingent on Cuban approval. The visit occurred amid Cuba's fuel-reserves-at-zero energy crisis.

**On follow-up cabinet visits — the central question:** **No specific follow-up visit to Cuba by another US cabinet official has been publicly announced or scheduled.** Secretary of State Rubio leads Cuba policy but his direct contact was a February sideline meeting in St. Kitts, not a Havana visit. US delegations to Cuba itself have been sub-cabinet (assistant secretaries, foreign-assistance officials in April). By late June 2026, talks had stalled ("no progress" per Cuba's FM), and the US shifted toward escalating sanctions rather than higher-level diplomacy.

Note: The AskNews tools were unavailable (rate/credit limits), so findings are drawn from web searches and direct article fetches across CBS, NPR, Axios, Al Jazeera, PBS, Foreign Policy, NBC, and CNN.
